sections.label: Lyrics
lines: [Verse 1 – soft ambient intro]
We carry fire in brittle bone
The breath of stars, but not our own
This dust remembers ancient skies
And we are vessels cracked by time
The light leaks out where we are thin
But that's where mercy rushes in
Not by our might, nor strength we claim
But through the fracture, speaks His name
[Pre-Chorus – gentle build with strings]
So let me break, and let me bend
Not the end, just how it mends
[Chorus – layered vocals, haunting repetition]
We are treasures in jars of clay
Shaped by hands that made the day
Though the edges chip away
Glory finds its home in grey
We are treasures in jars of clay
[Verse 2 – deeper strings, atmospheric guitar rise]
I tried to shine with borrowed gold
Built thrones of ego, masks of old
But grace undoes what pride defends
It carves a path that never ends
Each flaw a lantern in the dark
A holy wound, a sacred mark
The cracks become the map I see
To where the light is setting me free
[Pre-Chorus – slight rhythm pulse introduced]
So let me be the lesser flame
That flickers still, in sacred name
[Chorus – fuller arrangement, more harmonic lift]
We are treasures in jars of clay
Worn and weathered, still we stay
What is fading makes a way
For a strength that won’t decay
We are treasures in jars of clay
[Bridge – stripped back to ambient hum and layered whisper-vocals]
Not broken... just bearing
Not flawless... but faring
What is hidden... declaring
Through the fragile, He's preparing
[Chorus – final iteration, swelling strings and ambient crescendo]
We are treasures in jars of clay
Crushed, not cursed—led through dismay
Pressed but never swept away
In the fracture, love will stay
We are treasures in jars of clay
[Outro – fading echoes, minimal guitar]
And through the cracks... we glow...
